levels.title: The Blank Slate
levels.narrative: The server's security system demands proof that you're a real sysadmin. It wants you to create a specific file as a test. If the file appears, the system will trust you.
levels.riddle:
The server speaks: 'Prove yourself to me,
Create a file called access_key — let it be.
No content needed, just the name will do,
A gentle touch creates something new.'

files.instructions.txt: Create a file called 'access_key' to prove you belong here.

hints: You need to create a new, empty file.
The 'touch' command creates a new file if it doesn't exist.

levels.title: Building Rooms
levels.narrative: Access granted! The server needs you to organize its chaos. Files are scattered everywhere. Start by creating a proper directory structure to sort things out.
levels.riddle:
The server's files are all a mess,
Build a room to fix this stress.
A directory named 'sorted' is what we need,
Create the folder, plant the seed.
hints: You need to create a new directory (folder).
The 'mkdir' command makes a new directory.

levels.title: Nested Construction
levels.narrative: Good, but the server needs a deeper structure. It wants you to create an entire path of directories at once — a vault within a vault within a vault.
levels.riddle:
Three rooms deep, a path must be laid,
data/backup/2003 — the structure displayed.
Create them all in a single command's might,
A special flag lets you build the path outright.
hints: You need to create multiple nested directories at once.
mkdir alone won't work for nested paths — you need a flag for 'parents'.

levels.title: Demolition
levels.narrative: A corrupted file is causing the server to malfunction! The file is infected with bad data and must be destroyed before it spreads.
levels.riddle:
A virus lurks in a file called 'malware.dat',
Remove it quickly — squash it flat!
Delete the threat with a simple command,
Save the server from the villain's hand.
hints: You need to delete a specific file, but be careful not to delete the wrong ones!
The 'rm' command removes files. Use it with the filename.

levels.title: Clean Sweep
levels.narrative: The malware left behind an entire directory of junk data. It's a whole tree of corrupted files that must be purged. A simple rm won't work on directories...
levels.riddle:
A folder full of digital rot,
'junk_data' holds a tangled knot.
Remove the tree, from root to leaf,
A recursive flag brings sweet relief.
hints: You need to delete an entire directory and everything inside it.
The rm command needs a recursive flag to remove directories.
